channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Qatari Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani Dies at 74; Pakistan Observes Day of Mourning
item.description: The article reports the death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the former emir of Qatar, at the age of 74. It highlights his significant role in transforming Qatar into a global economic and poli...

item.title: Lagos Police Collaborate with Stakeholders to Address Mile 2–Apapa Traffic Congestion
item.description: The Ports Authority Police (Western Command) has clarified they are not directly responsible for the persistent gridlock along the Mile 2–Apapa corridor, stating their jurisdiction is limited to port ...

item.title: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham’s Death Creates Senate Vacancy Amid Political Uncertainty
item.description: The sudden death of longtime South Carolina Republican Senator Lindsey Graham has created an unexpected vacancy in the U.S. Senate, complicating President Donald Trump's legislative agenda. Graham, a ...

item.title: Microsoft's Datacenter Expansion Leads to 25% Rise in Emissions Despite Renewable Energy Goals
item.description: Microsoft's 2026 Environmental Sustainability Report reveals a significant increase in greenhouse gas emissions, rising by 25% from 13 million tons of CO2 equivalent in 2020 to 20 million tons in 2025...

item.title: Philippines and Saudi Arabia Collaborate on Combating Human Trafficking
item.description: The Bureau of Immigration in the Philippines has engaged in knowledge exchange with a high-level Saudi Arabian delegation to address human trafficking and transnational crimes. The Saudi delegation, w...

item.title: Golden emblem appears on Las Vegas billboards sparking speculation over Golden Knights anniversary
item.description: A cryptic golden symbol accompanied by the date 16 July has appeared on digital billboards across Las Vegas, prompting widespread curiosity among residents and visitors alike. The emblem has been spot...

item.title: Truckers Discuss Impact of Gordie Howe Bridge on Transportation
item.description: The article explores the concerns of truckers regarding the Gordie Howe Bridge, a critical infrastructure project connecting Windsor and Detroit. Truckers argue that the bridge's construction and oper...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Escalation and Hormuz Closure Intensify Regional Tensions
item.description: The article details the escalating military conflict between the United States and Iran, with U.S. strikes on Iranian targets and retaliatory attacks by Iran on Arab nations. The closure of the Strait...

item.title: Tanzania Under International Scrutiny as Global Community Examines Governance and Policies
item.description: Tanzania is currently facing renewed international scrutiny as global observers examine its governance, economic policies, and social developments. This attention highlights the nation's role in regio...

item.title: Two Men Killed While Rescuing Children at Hartlepool Beach
item.description: Two men have tragically lost their lives while attempting to rescue children in distress at Hartlepool beach. The incident occurred on Seaton Carew Beach in County Durham, where emergency services, in...

item.title: Tantita's Security Measures Enhance Niger Delta Stability for Economic Growth
item.description: The article highlights how Tantita's security operations in the Niger Delta are playing a crucial role in stabilizing the region and supporting Nigeria's economic goals. By securing oil pipelines and ...

item.title: Youths Tortured and Given Electric Shocks Over Theft Suspicion in Madhya Pradesh
item.description: Two youths from Dhaniyakhedi village in Madhya Pradesh's Raisen district were allegedly tortured by a mob over suspicion of stealing a water pump motor. The incident occurred on July 4 in Karmodiya vi...

item.title: PPP Condemns India's Threats to Pakistan's Water Share with Nationwide Rallies
item.description: The Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) organized large-scale rallies across Sindh to protest India's unilateral attempts to suspend the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) and its plans to divert Pakistani waters. Th...

item.title: South Korea Seeks North Korean Cooperation in Search for Missing Navy Seaman Near Border
item.description: On July 12, 2026, South Korea's unification ministry requested North Korea's assistance in locating a navy seaman who went missing during coast guard duties on the East Sea. The incident occurred near...

item.title: Philippine Military Academy Secures P379 Million Grant for Modern Warfare Lab
item.description: The Philippine Military Academy (PMA) has secured a P379.48-million funding grant from the Bloomberry Cultural Foundation Inc. (BCFI) to construct a modern warfare laboratory, part of Pagcor's corpora...

item.title: Ogun Police Successfully Raid Criminal Hideouts, Arrest 43 Suspects in Operation Kosaye
item.description: The Ogun State Police Command carried out a successful raid on criminal hideouts in Isheri-Olofin, leading to the arrest of 43 suspects as part of Operation Kosaye. The operation, launched jointly wit...

item.title: White House Insiders Identify Potential Replacement for Lindsey Graham as Trump's Key Liaison
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died suddenly on Saturday night from a 'brief and sudden illness,' according to his office, marking a significant loss for the Trump administration. White House insiders hav...

item.title: Colombian Footballer Faces Safety Concerns After World Cup Exit
item.description: Colombian footballer Jaminton Campaz has decided not to return to his home country following Colombia's elimination from the 2026 World Cup qualifiers. The striker received death threats, prompting hi...

item.title: British-Zimbabwean suspect faces South African court after arrest in Johannesburg
item.description: Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a British-Zimbabwean man wanted in the UK for allegedly murdering his wife and two daughters, is set to appear in a South African court following his arrest in Johannesburg. ...

item.title: UN Warns of Catastrophic Consequences from US-Iran Strikes
item.description: The United Nations secretary-general, António Guterres, has urged restraint from all parties involved in escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, warning of imminent 'catastrophic conse...
